steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence lists three measurement units — the sa, the shesep, and the khar — each followed by a comma and a description (e.g., “a unit of area”). Because each list item already contains an internal comma, the items must be separated by semicolons, not commas, to prevent ambiguity. The blank must supply the correct punctuation between sa’s description and the shesep item, and again between the shesep item and khar.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — area; the shesep; a unit of length;: Places a semicolon between shesep and “a unit of length,” splitting the name from its own description. That breaks the structure of the shesep item. ✗
  • Choice B — area the shesep, a unit of length: No punctuation after “area” runs the end of the sa item directly into the shesep item, creating a run-on. ✗
  • Choice C — area; the shesep, a unit of length;: A semicolon after “area” correctly separates the sa item from the shesep item; a comma correctly links shesep with “a unit of length”; and a semicolon after “length” correctly separates the shesep item from the final khar item. ✓
  • Choice D — area, the shesep; a unit of length,: Uses a comma after “area” (insufficient to separate items that already contain commas) and places a semicolon between shesep and its description, again splitting the name from its descriptor. ✗
C is correct.
